title: coelogyne cootesii
intro: Coelogyne cootesii, also known as the Cootes' Coelogyne, is a species of orchid native to the Himalayas and parts of Asia. It is an epiphytic orchid, meaning it grows on other plants or objects for support, and is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Indian medicine. The roots of the Coelogyne cootesii plant are used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. In herbal preparations, the roots are often dried and made into a powder or decoction to be used in treatments, and have been found to have potential health benefits in reducing inflammation and improving respiratory health.
